content.principles: 1. Direct Buffer (off-heap memory) to avoid copying between kernel and user space. 2. CompositeByteBuf (logical combination of buffers). 3. FileRegion/transferTo (OS level zero-copy). 4. Wrap existing byte arrays without copying.
content.pitfalls: Direct memory leaks if not properly released. Complexity in managing direct buffer lifecycles. transferTo might not work for all file systems/OS.
